accurate.why: All claims are supported by the bill text or accurate context (SBA-administered SBIC program, Small Business Investment Act of 1958).

no_hallucinations.why: No fabricated claims; the SBIC abbreviation and program description are accurate context, not contradictions.

states_purpose.why: Opening clearly states the bill amends the SBIC leverage limit framework under the 1958 Act.

changes_to_existing_law.why: Correctly identifies that it amends the Small Business Investment Act of 1958, including private capital definition and leverage caps.

exceptions_conditions.why: Notes the carve-out for government funds (except specified subclauses) and the lesser-of-50%-or-$125M exclusion cap and 200% limit retention.

effective_dates.why: Correctly notes only investments made after the May 19, 2026 enactment date are eligible for the exclusion.

major_provisions_covered.why: Captures the private capital redefinition, the 300%-to-200% reduction, the dollar caps, and the expanded bonus-leverage exclusion.

objective_tone.why: Neutral, descriptive language with no advocacy or editorializing.

coherent.why: Well-organized into logical paragraphs that are internally consistent and clear.

concise.why: Compact with no padding or repetition.

correct_entities.why: Correctly names the SBIC program, the 1958 Act, and accurately describes the affected categories and definitions.

correct_figures.why: All figures (300%/200%, $250M, $175M, $475M, $350M, 50%/$125M, date) match the bill text exactly.
